basicDetails.implementingAgency: Uttarakhand Building And Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Family Pension Scheme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCWWB) under the Labour Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the spouse of a registered construction worker in the event of the worker’s death, if the deceased was a pensioner.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - **Pension Amount:** ₹500/- per month or 50% of the pensioner's pension, whichever is higher.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme provides financial assistance to the spouse of a registered construction worker in the event of the worker’s death, if the deceased was a pensioner. Under this scheme, the family pension will be ₹500/- per month or 50% of the pensioner's pension, whichever is higher.

children.text: The scheme “Family Pension Scheme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCWWB) under the Labour Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to the spouse of a registered construction worker in the event of the worker’s death, if the deceased was a pensioner.
₹500/- per month or 50% of the pensioner's pension, whichever is higher.
The online application for the scheme can be submitted through Shramik Suvidha Kendras.

At the time of application, the applicant must attach the self-declaration certificate, Shramik card of the deceased worker, death certificate of the deceased worker, Aadhaar card, and other required documents.

The application for family pension must be submitted within three months from the date of death of the pensioner. If the application is found correct during the departmental verification, the family pension will be sanctioned and disbursed.

applicationProcess.process_md: - The online application for the scheme can be submitted through Shramik Suvidha Kendras. - At the time of application, the applicant must attach the self-declaration certificate, Shramik card of the deceased worker, death certificate of the deceased worker, Aadhaar card, and other required documents. - The application for family pension must be submitted within three months from the date of death of the pensioner. If the application is found correct during the departmental verification, the family pension will be sanctioned and disbursed.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be the spouse (husband/wife) of the deceased construction worker. 1. The deceased construction worker must have been registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board. 1. The deceased construction worker must have been receiving a pension from the Board at the time of death.

children.text: The applicant must be the spouse (husband/wife) of the deceased construction worker.
The deceased construction worker must have been registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
The deceased construction worker must have been receiving a pension from the Board at the time of death.
